desc_short: Not a faction. An ecology with teeth.
desc_long: The canyon was here first. Monsters are not an army — they are the reason Coffin Canyon exists. Native inhabitants who react to intruders with instinct, territory, and survival.
history: After the Catastrophe, the canyon's hidden layers woke up. Tainted ground drew hungry things. Haunted ground gathered the bloodless. Strangewild growth fed predators. Every extraction, every fire, every desperate camp — another invitation. The Monsters faction represents what happens when the canyon gets a vote.

what_they_fight_for: Protection of nests, warrens, and lairs
what_they_fight_against: Anyone who thinks the canyon belongs to them

reputation.allies_see_them_as: A volatile third party — useful chaos if you can survive it.
reputation.enemies_see_them_as: A constant threat and a shifting nightmare: too many kinds, too many rules.

faction_mechanics.playstyle: A toolbox of threats: cheap objective pests, stealth assassins, terror pieces, durable anchors, and occasional Titans. Monsters excel at disrupting plans, punishing overextension, and forcing Morale tests and positional mistakes.
strengths: Wide spread of roles and stat profiles
High ceiling with ambush and terrain play
Titans and large threats can swing games
weaknesses: Some units are fragile and rely on timing and cover
Faction identity is threat-based, not objective-based by default

preferred_states.reason: Predators thrive, nests multiply, and the canyon grows teeth.
Bloodless and spirits become common, and the living make worse decisions.
Toxic weather and tainted ground favor the things that were built for it.

neutral_states.reason: Chaos invites monsters, but chaos also brings more guns.
Containment reduces roaming threats, but lairs still exist.

opposed_states.reason: Order, suppression, and industrial discipline reduce monster roaming and leverage.
Strip-mined wastelands remove the ecosystem — some monsters starve out, others escalate.
Machine dominance and dead zones reduce living ecology.

ideal_scenarios: Escort, redirect, and calm monster scenarios
Territory control with lairs and nests

faction_features.name: Native Deployment
faction_features.effect: Monsters deploy after all terrain is placed, but before other factions. They may deploy anywhere on the table, including inside ruins, near Thyr crystals, or in ambush positions.

faction_features.name: Monster Exits
faction_features.effect: Scenarios may designate Monster Exits (mines, caves, ruins, sewers, hidden paths). A Monster may leave the table via an Exit instead of activating normally. Exited Monsters may score Survival or Escape VP (scenario dependent).

faction_features.name: Temperament System
faction_features.effect: Every Monster has a Temperament (Friendly, Vengeful, Cunning, Territorial, Skittish, Stalwart) that affects how it reacts to danger and morale. See individual unit cards for specific behaviors.

faction_features.name: Befriending
faction_features.effect: Certain Monsters (CanBeBefriended) may be befriended, escorted, or temporarily allied with Monster Rangers. Others (CannotBeBefriended) react violently to such attempts.

victory_objectives.description: Monsters score VP for remaining alive at game end. Specific values are scenario-dependent.
Monsters score VP for remaining near nests, ruins, lairs, or designated territories.
Monsters score VP for holding or consuming Thyr crystals.
Monsters score VP for successfully exiting the table via Monster Exits.
Only certain predatory Monsters score VP for kills.
Some Monsters grant points to killers or cause global VP shifts when destroyed.

composition_requirements.note: Monsters are scenario-driven and do not follow standard army composition rules. Scenarios determine which Monsters appear, how many, and their victory conditions.
composition_requirements.deployment_order: Monsters deploy after terrain placement but before other factions.
composition_requirements.titan_rule: Titans are scenario events. When a Titan enters play, all factions must reassess priorities.

weapons_note: Monster costs assume natural weapons and profile abilities.

units.name: Canyon Goblin
units.lore: Canyon goblins scavenge old rails, broken wagons, and ruined camps. They know every crack in the stone, every shade line at noon, and every hiding place worth having.
units.tactics: Objective nuisance. Use them to screen, contest, and flee. They are not fighters — they are a problem that requires a response. The Red Goblin variant is a different beast entirely: faster, meaner, and impossible to befriend.

supplemental_abilities.name: Red Goblin
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Circus goblins wearing the red hat and iron shoes. Fast, cruel, and difficult to pin down.

units.name: Jackalope
units.lore: The jackalope is the most famous lie ever told in the canyon — and somehow it keeps proving itself real. Horned rabbits ricochet across the desert floor faster than bullets, leaving only dust and disbelief behind.
units.tactics: Objective thief and battlefield chaos agent. Move 12" with Fast means this unit reaches objectives before almost anything else. Wild Ricochet lets it bounce to a second target after attacking. Thyr-Touched Antlers adds Fear for minimal cost — suddenly a small fast thing is also terrifying. Dust Mirage lets it dodge the one shot that would have ended it.

optional_upgrades.name: Thyr-Touched Antlers
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain fear. Antlers glow faintly with Thyr energy.

optional_upgrades.name: Dust Mirage
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per round when targeted by a ranged attack, may move 3" before the attack is resolved.

supplemental_abilities.name: Alpha Jackalope
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A massive horned jackalope rumored to lead desert herds. When it charges, it hits like a flying spear.

units.name: Canyon Harpy
units.lore: Canyon harpies roost in the high red cliffs, building nests of bones, broken bottles, and stolen jewelry. Their feathers stink of rot and carrion, and their claws carry the sickness of everything they have eaten.
units.tactics: Fast harassment predator. Fly over screens, strike weak targets, and retreat before heavier units respond. Venom compounds damage over time — prioritize lightly armored targets where the delayed damage matters. Rotwing Harpy is a nastier version with Fear and Choking Dust, good for disrupting clustered formations.

supplemental_abilities.name: Rotwing Harpy
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A diseased canyon harpy whose wings drip infection. Gains fear, venom, and choking_dust.

units.name: Ridge Satyr
units.lore: Ridge satyrs haunt the dry groves and hidden springs of Coffin Canyon. With cracked pan pipes and crooked smiles, they play tunes that slip into the bones and make good sense wander off somewhere else.
units.tactics: Control rogue. Hex dangerous enemies and disrupt formations with pipe-song. Avoid direct combat — satyrs win by mischief rather than strength. Brass Bow extends threat range to 18" and makes them able to Hex from safety. Moonridge Satyr adds Fear for stronger morale disruption.

optional_upgrades.name: Brass Bow
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Add a Brass Bow (Range 18"). A strange mechanical bow of brass limbs that hums faintly when drawn.

supplemental_abilities.name: Moonridge Satyr
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A pale satyr that wanders the canyon by moonlight. Gains fear.

units.name: Vendomat

composition.note: Scenario-placed only. Not drafted into rosters.

units.lore: The Vendomat appeared one morning without explanation — ten feet of clanking iron and carved pumpkin, wandering the canyon floor with a stock of supplies and a coin slot in its chest. Nobody knows who built it. Nobody attacks it twice.
units.tactics: The Vendomat wanders at Move 6". Any faction can spend an action and 2 Gildren to buy supplies — first come, first served. Stock is limited. It fights back if attacked, and its death explosion punishes the whole board. Smart players leave it alone and race to reach it.

special_mechanics.final_dispensing: When the Vendomat is destroyed, resolve a 10" Blast centered on it before removing it from play.

units.name: Monte Haul

composition.note: Scenario-placed only. Not drafted into rosters.

units.lore: Monte Haul is a brass mechan in the shape of a broad-shouldered merchant, spring-mounted to a heavy base. He sells things that probably shouldn't exist. He takes Gildren only. He gives no receipts. He has never moved from a spot once planted. Nobody has robbed him successfully.
units.tactics: Placed at scenario start and stays there all game. Both factions can trade — whoever gets there first has the advantage. D5 plus ignoring first 2 damage per activation means he is very hard to destroy. He goes into Lockdown at low health rather than dying. Do not waste activations trying to kill him.

special_mechanics.black_market: Any faction may spend an action and 2 Gildren adjacent to Monte Haul to purchase one item from his stock. Stock is defined by scenario. Monte Haul has limited supply — once an item is sold, it is gone.
special_mechanics.lockdown_protocol: When Monte Haul is reduced to Q1, he enters Lockdown: cannot be targeted by attacks, cannot be traded with, and cannot be moved. He remains on the table as difficult terrain.

units.name: Canyon Skeleton
units.lore: No one buried them right, and now the canyon is their business. Canyon Skeletons rise from collapsed mine shafts, dry riverbeds, and unmarked graves wherever the ground has been disturbed enough to let something out.
units.tactics: Pressure through persistence. They keep coming. Relentless means every kill earns a free 2" surge, and Fearless means no Morale test ever slows them down. Field them in pairs or small clusters so rattling_horde fires consistently. Send them at objectives and make enemies spend resources removing them.

optional_upgrades.name: Ancient Blade
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain pierce. The blade pre-dates most of the armor it encounters.

optional_upgrades.name: Buckler Shield
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per round, when hit by a melee attack, reduce the damage by 1 (minimum 0).

optional_upgrades.name: Dread Rattling
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain fear. The sound it makes — all that grinding bone and scraping metal — is something the brain rejects.

optional_upgrades.name: Canyon Dirt Armor
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain tough. Centuries of compressed canyon sediment have filled every joint and sealed every crack.

supplemental_abilities.name: Skeleton Archer
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Some skeletons rose mid-draw and never put the bow down. Lose relentless, gain 12" ranged attack.

supplemental_abilities.name: Skeleton Champion
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: An old officer that went into the ground with its rank still on. Tougher, more frightening, and holds nearby skeletons in a loose and terrible formation.

units.name: Dog Spider
units.lore: Dog spiders are canyon ambush predators the size of wolves. They skitter across rocks and wagon trails alike, striking from shadows before retreating into cracks in the stone.
units.tactics: Fast ambusher. Strike isolated targets and retreat before heavy units respond. Venom adds delayed damage that makes even a failed kill worthwhile. Web Spitter gives Pinning at range — combine it with a charge next round. Burrower is a once-per-game repositioning tool that can surprise the entire enemy line.

optional_upgrades.name: Web Spitter
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain web spit attack (Range 6"). On a successful hit, target becomes Pinned.

optional_upgrades.name: Burrower
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per game, may emerge anywhere within 8" of starting position, not within 3" of an enemy.

supplemental_abilities.name: Thyrfang Spider
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A Dog Spider warped by feeding on Thyr crystals. Fangs glow faintly. Gains fear, venom, and furious.

units.name: Feratu
units.lore: Tall, pale blood drinkers hiding behind civility and shadow. In Coffin Canyon they hunt selectively, feeding on the living and growing bolder with every kill.
units.tactics: A scaling predator. Early game: isolate Grunts and wounded targets for Blood Feast dice. Late game: those accumulated dice turn this unit into something that tears through high-Quality targets. Piercing Bite and Brutal Blow are both strong upgrades once the Feratu is in its stride. Thyr Frenzy is extremely dangerous if timed correctly.

optional_upgrades.name: Thyr Frenzy
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain thyr_devourer and thyr_frenzy. Quality cannot exceed 6.

supplemental_abilities.name: Feratu Stalker
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Feral night-hunter. Gains fast. Even Skull Rangers cannot befriend it.

supplemental_abilities.name: Feratu Bride
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A charming predator that draws victims close before feeding. Gains brutal_fear.

supplemental_abilities.name: Feratu Gentleman
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: An aristocratic hunter who studies prey before striking. Gains mark_target. Quality cannot exceed 6.

units.name: Golem
units.lore: Constructs stitched, sculpted, or forged into life by the strange sciences of Coffin Canyon. Some are academic experiments, others relics of industry, and some simply refuse to stop moving.
units.tactics: Durable front-line anchor. Choose a material variant to define how the Golem behaves. Flesh Golem adds Relentless and Fear for aggressive pressure. Clay Golem upgrades to Tougher for near-impenetrable blocking. Iron Golem has Toughest and drops Spare Parts on death — even killing it benefits you.

supplemental_abilities.name: Flesh Golem
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Grotesque monsterologist construct. Gains relentless and fear.

supplemental_abilities.name: Clay Golem
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Shaped from canyon clay. Gains tougher and fearless.

supplemental_abilities.name: Iron Golem
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Brutal iron construct. Gains toughest, fearless, and spare_parts_drop.

units.name: Halloween Golem
units.lore: Obsolete mechanical pumpkin-headed men built to bring order to the wilderness — cutting trees and tending farms. Now they wander about looking for trees to accost, and Thyr.
units.tactics: Reliable blockers that grind forward and absorb punishment while leaving salvage behind. Keen Axe adds Pierce for a minimal cost — worth it against armored targets. Rusty Halloween Golem is a cheaper, weaker version for scenarios where budget matters more than staying power.

supplemental_abilities.name: Rusty Halloween Golem
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A rusting piece of trash held together by habit and hope.

units.name: Crag Thrallo
units.lore: Once forced to haul stone, iron, and scrap for cruel masters, Thrallos eventually learned that chains break easier than bones. Now they roam junkyards and gravel pits, smashing machines apart and drinking whatever strange brew they find in rusted barrels.
units.tactics: Forward bruiser. Charge into the center, absorb punishment, and smash anything fragile. Impact plus Rebar Club makes charges extremely punishing. Scrap Armor brings Defense to 5 — very difficult to shift. Doomshine Thrallo is faster and hits harder but cannot control its movement, which is a real tactical challenge.

optional_upgrades.name: Rebar Club
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain brutal_blow. A length of twisted construction steel makes every hit devastating.

supplemental_abilities.name: Doomshine Thrallo
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Fueled by stolen Doomshine. Gains +1 Quality and furious, but must move toward the nearest enemy each activation if able.

units.name: Gulch Minotaur
units.lore: A bull-headed ruin-dweller that knows every corridor in the old stoneworks. If it charges, it's because you're already lost.
units.tactics: Charge specialist. Use terrain density to set up devastating Impact lines through corridors and ruins. Brutal Impact plus Brutal Blow means a charge deals 2 automatic hits before dice and then hits harder than average. Huge Battle Axe adds Pierce for armored targets. Labyrinth Bull adds Relentless for grinding follow-ups.

supplemental_abilities.name: Labyrinth Bull
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Years of stalking ruins have honed its instincts. After destroying an enemy in melee, may immediately move 2". Gains relentless.

units.name: Patchwork Wonder
units.lore: A creature of many parts, sewn together with string and wire and sparked to life by aethyr. Sometimes cloth. Sometimes muscle and gristle. Always strange.
units.tactics: Close in fast, hit hard, and keep pressure. Relentless means every kill fuels another surge. Furious means every hit taken fuels the next attack. Patchwork thrives in messy multi-unit fights where both abilities trigger repeatedly. Aethyr Surge adds Hex for a small cost. Sackcloth Patchwork is a cheaper fragile version for budget rosters.

supplemental_abilities.name: Sackcloth Patchwork
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A cheap and unstable cloth-stuffed construct.

units.name: Owlbear
units.lore: A hulking canyon predator with the body of a bear and the eyes of a hunting owl. When it screeches across the gulch walls, even seasoned Rangers remember they are prey.
units.tactics: Shock predator. Charge into exposed enemies and break lines with Impact and brutal melee. Move 8" means it can reach most targets in one activation. Iron-Breaking Talons adds Pierce — very strong against Brine Divers and armored Liberty Corps units. Blood Scent adds Furious for games where it expects to take damage.

supplemental_abilities.name: Mama Owlbear
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Territorial mother defending her young. Gains +1 Defense and furious.

supplemental_abilities.name: Baby Owlbear
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A young unpredictable owlbear cub. Fast, loud, and prone to panic.

units.name: Chimera
units.lore: A stitched-together legend that shouldn't survive in the dry valley — lion muscle, goat stubbornness, and serpent spite. Yet it prowls Coffin Canyon anyway, breathing dust and eating regrets.
units.tactics: Centerpiece brute. Push it into the center of the fight where Furious triggers repeatedly. Q6 means it rarely misses. Fear keeps enemies making Morale tests every round. Serpent Tail adds Poison for lingering damage. Infernal Breath is a 3-use cone that can clear entire formations. Alpha Chimera adds +1 Defense for a unit that already invites focus fire.

optional_upgrades.name: Serpent Tail
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain venom. A venomous serpent head lashes from the creature's tail.

optional_upgrades.name: Infernal Breath
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain hell_spray. Three-use cone attack of burning aethyr.

supplemental_abilities.name: Alpha Chimera
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Ancient canyon apex predator with scarred hide. Gains +1 Defense and furious.

units.name: Dust King
units.lore: Ancient bandaged dead-men, risen from forbidden chemical ruins. Dust Kings wander out of collapsed tombs and sunken chambers, chewing fresh flesh when they find it.
units.tactics: Slow, durable melee pressure. Park them in ruins — Ruinbound gives +1 Defense in their natural habitat, making them extremely hard to shift. Fear grinds enemy Morale down over time. Tougher means damage rolls frequently fail. Brutal Fear and Toughest upgrades push an already tough unit into genuinely threatening territory.

optional_upgrades.name: Thyr Frenzy
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Gain thyr_devourer and thyr_frenzy. Quality cannot exceed 6.

supplemental_abilities.name: Dust King Revenant
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: More powerful risen form. Gains tomb_curse: engaged enemies must test Morale after their activation.

supplemental_abilities.name: Dust Emperor
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: The apex Bloodless commander. Gains royal_tomb_ward and dust_command.

units.name: Kelpie
units.lore: A damp horse-beast out of place in the dry canyon. Desperate, thirsty, and dangerous — pulling prey into shade, mud, or deeper trouble.
units.tactics: Hit-and-run controller. Grapple key targets off objectives or into danger zones. Water Lurker gives +1 Defense near water, and Dry Desperation lets it sacrifice Health for speed on sand terrain. Works best near water terrain but can function anywhere — the Grapple effect is useful regardless of terrain. River Kelpie is tougher and faster; Starved Kelpie is cheaper but erratic.

supplemental_abilities.name: River Kelpie
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Strong river hunter with greater endurance. Gains fast and +1 Defense.

supplemental_abilities.name: Starved Kelpie
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A desperate desert wanderer running on instinct and spite. Gains furious.

units.name: Lionvulture
units.lore: A four-legged wild beast — more animal than intellect — nesting near Thyr outcroppings where it can guard and feed.
units.tactics: Flank, pounce, and retreat. Move 10" plus Fast covers almost the entire board in one turn. Use it to contest Thyr crystals and punish isolated models that wander too close. Thyr Guardian rewards positioning near crystals — an objective it was already going to defend. Savage Pounce adds Impact for charges; Blood Scent adds Furious for sustained fights.

supplemental_abilities.name: Alpha Lionvulture
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Dominant territorial predator leading the pack. Gains fear and +1 Quality.

units.name: Valley Centaur
units.lore: Nomads of the high mesas, valley centaurs roam the broken trails and wind-cut ridges of Coffin Canyon. Some will bargain for water, salt, or news. Most prefer to run strangers off with a warning arrow.
units.tactics: Mobile skirmisher. Suppress dangerous enemies from range then reposition quickly to stay out of melee. Longbow extends range to 18" — excellent for suppressing from safety. Bloodhoof Centaur is a completely different animal: an ogre-centaur with Brutal Impact, Fear, and Relentless that functions as a Brute in cavalry clothing.

supplemental_abilities.name: Bloodhoof Centaur
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Enormous ogre-centaur that charges like a living avalanche. Gains fear, brutal_impact, and relentless.

units.name: Lamia
units.lore: A mirage given teeth. Lamias haunt the dry places where trails fade and water rumors begin. Travelers who follow their laughter often vanish into the heat shimmer.
units.tactics: Control and attrition. Hex the most dangerous enemy each round. Brain Bloom hits through Cover, which is excellent against entrenched positions. Mirage Veil gives a once-per-round dodge against ranged attacks. Hypnotic Eyes adds Fear for morale pressure. Dune Lamia is a stronger version with Q5 and Fear built in.

optional_upgrades.name: Mirage Veil
optional_upgrades.desc_short: Once per round when targeted by a ranged attack, may move 3" before the attack resolves.

supplemental_abilities.name: Dune Lamia
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Ancient Lamia that has ruled the dunes for decades. Gains fear and +1 Quality.

units.name: Medusa
units.lore: A ruin-queen who wanders the shattered temples and dry river shrines of Coffin Canyon. Her gaze does not kill outright — it turns courage into hesitation and flesh slowly into stone. The canyon is littered with statues that once had names.
units.tactics: Debuff controller. Keep distance and maintain line of sight while stacking Petrify Tokens on priority targets. 1 token slows movement, 2 tokens Pin each activation, 3 tokens fully immobilize. Combined with Fear, Medusa can make an entire flank functionally useless without dealing a point of damage.

units.name: Bête Herdbeast
units.lore: Huge, shaggy horned beasts that smell faintly of candy. Bêtes are dream-made giants — often gentle — yet terrifying when startled.
units.tactics: Anchor the center. Use its footprint to block lanes and protect Thyr. Avoid unnecessary fights; win by surviving, holding ground, or being Escorted. Gentle Giant means Monster Rangers can protect it without triggering retaliation. Stampede is a double-edged sword — a panicked Bête stampedes through everyone. Mama Bête is more aggressive and rushes toward anything threatening her calf.

supplemental_abilities.name: Gentle Giant
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: Will not declare attacks against Monster Rangers unless attacked first.

supplemental_abilities.name: Stampede
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: If this unit fails a Morale test it immediately moves up to its Move in a straight line, passing through smaller models and applying impact normally.

supplemental_abilities.name: Baby Bête
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A small, skittish Bête calf still learning the world is dangerous.

supplemental_abilities.name: Mama Bête
supplemental_abilities.desc_short: A fierce mother. Gains toughest, brutal_impact, and brutal_fear. When a Baby Bête within 6" is targeted, she may immediately move up to 4" toward the attacker.

units.name: Klowna
units.weapon: Titan Talons, Twin Maw & Triple-Leg Lunge
units.lore: A towering chitinous horror — nearly 14 feet tall — with two heads, three legs, and enormous talons. It crawls out of an old wreck like a bad joke that refuses to end.
units.tactics: A shared catastrophe. Spawn it mid-game to spike tension and force everyone to reassess. Twin Maw splits attacks between two targets. Two Heads rolls each activation for unpredictable behavior. Down the Hatch destroys anything it significantly outrolls in melee. Savage Fear radiates dread across the whole board. The goal is disruption — Klowna wins by making everyone react to it.

notes: Undead and Incorporeal monsters marked SkullRangerBefriendOnly may only be Befriended by the Monster Rangers Skull Ranger branch.
Titans are scenario events. When a Titan enters play, all factions must reassess priorities.
Temperament is used by the scenario engine to determine monster AI behavior when uncontrolled.
Bloodless monsters are immune to Poison and Blood in the Air effects.
